text: In phenomenological philosophy, Edmund Husserl distinguished between the 'living body' (Leib) — experienced from within — and the 'objective body' (Körper) — observed from outside. Neuroscience has since mapped this distinction onto distinct cortical circuits. The insula and anterior cingulate process Leib-like signals: ownership, effort, warmth, the sense that this body is mine. Proprioceptive and visual cortices process Körper-like signals: shape, position, movement in space. Attention-training traditions that anchor awareness in breath or heartbeat operate in the Leib domain. They do not merely calm the body; they relocate the attentional center of gravity from abstract thought to inhabited presence.
question: According to the passage, what is the key distinction between the cortical circuits processing Leib and those processing Körper?

options.text: Leib circuits involve the insula and cingulate processing ownership and inner felt experience; Körper circuits involve position and movement in space
Leib circuits process voluntary movement; Körper circuits process involuntary sensation
Leib circuits are older evolutionarily; Körper circuits are uniquely human cortical developments
Leib circuits require conscious effort to activate; Körper circuits operate entirely below awareness
